plainEnglish: At Level AAA, the keyboard focus indicator must be substantial: at least as large as a 2px-thick outline around the element, and with at least 3:1 contrast between the focused and unfocused states. A thin, faint focus ring isn't enough—make it large and high-contrast.
whyItMatters: Users with low vision can't perceive a thin or low-contrast focus indicator and lose track of their keyboard position.
quickCheck: Inspect your focus styles and confirm the indicator is at least 2 CSS px thick (or equivalent area) with at least 3:1 contrast against the unfocused state.
commonMistakes: A 1px focus outline that's too thin to meet the minimum area.
A focus color with less than 3:1 contrast against the component's normal state.
Relying on a subtle color shift instead of a clearly visible outline.

codeExamples.label: Thin, low-contrast focus indicator
